ground_truth.description: Changed 'merged = base.copy()' to 'merged = base' (removed the copy). This means _deep_merge now mutates the original base dict passed by the caller. Since the function modifies 'merged' in-place via merged[key] = value, every call to _deep_merge will permanently alter the input base dict. This causes config contamination — loading config with overrides will permanently modify the defaults dict, affecting all subsequent loads.
The updated docstring claims 'The caller's base dict is never mutated — we work on a copy' but the implementation now assigns merged = base directly (no copy), contradicting the documented behavior.
